posts.title: Getting Started with EEG Preprocessing
posts.excerpt: A practical checklist for cleaning EEG signals before machine learning and analysis.
content: Preprocessing is where neuroscience projects become reliable. Clean signals are the difference between trustworthy conclusions and noisy guesses.
Start with a clear pipeline: re-reference, filter, remove artifacts, epoch the signal, and normalize when needed. Keep each stage documented and reproducible.
The most common mistake is applying aggressive filtering too early. Keep transformations minimal until you inspect your data quality visually and statistically.

posts.title: Designing a BCI Project Roadmap
posts.excerpt: How to structure a BCI project from data collection to model evaluation.
content: A strong BCI roadmap starts with one measurable goal: classification accuracy, latency, or user comfort. Pick one primary metric first.
Map your phases: task design, signal acquisition, preprocessing, feature engineering, modeling, and closed-loop testing. Each phase should have a clear exit criterion.
Publish your assumptions and failures. In neurotech, transparent iteration is often more valuable than a perfect first result.

posts.title: Open Datasets for Neuro-ML Projects
posts.excerpt: A shortlist of public datasets to build and benchmark neuroscience ML ideas.
content: Public datasets help you prototype faster and benchmark fairly. They are ideal for validating ideas before investing in custom data collection.
Choose datasets based on signal modality, subject count, and recording conditions. A small but clean dataset can outperform a larger, inconsistent one.
Document all preprocessing assumptions when comparing models across datasets to avoid hidden leakage and inflated scores.
